0.original: When you help others feel important, you help yourself feel important, too.
0.b1: When you make other people feel important, you also feel important yourself.
0.cn: 当你让别人感到重要时，你自己也会感到重要。
0.ai_notes: 'feel important': to believe that you are a valuable person.
0.remarks: 'feel important': 感到自己很重要，指认为自己是有价值的人。

1.original: One of the elevator operators who carried me up and down for several months had the look of complete unimportance written all over.
1.b1: One of the elevator operators who took me up and down for months looked like she felt unimportant.
1.cn: 有一位电梯操作员，几个月来一直负责载我上下楼，她看起来完全没有存在感。
1.ai_notes:
'elevator operator': a person whose job is to run an elevator.
'unimportance': the feeling of not being valuable or noticed.
'written all over': to be very obvious from someone's face or behavior.
1.remarks:
'elevator operator': 电梯操作员，负责运行电梯的人。
'unimportance': 不重要感，指感到自己没有价值或被忽视。
'written all over': 写在脸上，指从某人的表情或行为中显而易见。

2.original: She was fiftiesh, unattractive, and certainly uninspired in her work.
2.b1: She was about fifty, not very pretty, and she did not seem to enjoy her work.
2.cn: 她五十岁左右，相貌平平，工作也显得毫无热情。
2.ai_notes:
'fiftiesh': about fifty years old.
'uninspired': lacking interest, excitement, or creativity.
2.remarks:
'fiftiesh': 五十岁左右，指大约五十岁。
'uninspired': 缺乏灵感的，指缺乏兴趣、兴奋感或创造力。

3.original: It was obvious that her longing to be important was completely unfulfilled.
3.b1: It was clear that she wanted to feel important, but she did not.
3.cn: 很明显，她渴望被重视，但这种渴望从未得到满足。
3.ai_notes:
'longing': a strong feeling of wanting something.
'unfulfilled': not achieved or satisfied.
3.remarks:
'longing': 渴望，指想要某事物的强烈感觉。
'unfulfilled': 未实现的，指愿望未达成或感到不满足。

4.original: She was one of the millions of people who live for months at a time without ever being given a reason to believe that someone notices them or cares about them.
4.b1: She was one of many people who live for a long time without feeling that anyone notices or cares about them.
4.cn: 她是千千万万普通人中的一员，他们长年累月地生活着，却从未感觉到有人关注或关心他们。
4.ai_notes:
'live for months at a time': to continue living for a long period without change.
'notices': to see or pay attention to someone.
4.remarks:
'live for months at a time': 长时间维持现状，指长期保持某种生活状态而不改变。
'notices': 注意到，指看到或关注某人。

5.original: One morning, shortly after I became one of her regular uppers and downers,
5.b1: One morning, after I had been riding her elevator for a while,
5.cn: 一天早上，在我成为她的常客不久后，
5.ai_notes: 'regular uppers and downers': a playful way to describe someone who uses the elevator frequently.
5.remarks: 'regular uppers and downers': 经常上下楼的人，一种幽默的说法，指频繁使用电梯的人。

6.original: I noticed that she had had her hair redone.
6.b1: I noticed that she had changed her hairstyle.
6.cn: 我注意到她换了新发型。
6.ai_notes: 'redone': to have something changed or styled again.
6.remarks: 'redone': 重做的，指某物被重新设计或处理过。

7.remarks: 'fancy': 高级的，指昂贵、复杂或装饰华丽的。

8.original: It was obviously a homemade job.
8.b1: It looked like she did it herself at home.
8.cn: 很明显是她自己在家里打理的。
8.ai_notes: 'homemade job': something made by a person at home rather than by a professional.
8.remarks: 'homemade job': 自制品，指非专业人员在家中制作的东西。

9.original: But it had been cut and it did look better.
9.b1: But it was cut and looked better.
9.cn: 但修剪过后确实好看了一些。
9.ai_notes: 'cut': in this context, it means her hair was trimmed or styled.
9.remarks: 'cut': 修剪，在此语境下指头发被剪短或做了造型。

10.original: So I said, Miss S. Note, I had learned her name.
10.b1: So I said, "Miss S." (I had learned her name).
10.cn: 于是我说：“S小姐。”（我已经知道了她的名字）。
10.ai_notes: 'Note': a way to tell the reader to pay attention to a specific detail.
10.remarks: 'Note': 注意，提醒读者关注某个特定细节。

11.original: I do like what you've done to your hair.
11.b1: I really like your new hair.
11.ai_notes: 'what you've done to your hair': a common way to ask about or comment on a new hairstyle.
11.remarks: 'what you've done to your hair': 你头发怎么弄的，询问或评价新发型的常用语。

12.original: It really looks fine." She blushed, said, thank you, sir, and nearly missed her next stop.
12.b1: It looks great." She blushed, said thank you, and almost missed her floor.
12.cn: 它看起来真的很棒。”她脸红了，说了声谢谢，差点错过了下一站。
12.ai_notes:
'blushed': to become red in the face because of shyness or being pleased.
'nearly missed her next stop': almost forgot to stop the elevator at the correct floor.
12.remarks:
'blushed': 脸红，因害羞或高兴而脸变红。
'nearly missed her next stop': 差点错过下一站，指几乎忘记在正确的楼层停电梯。

13.b1: She was happy that I noticed.
13.remarks: 'appreciated': 被感激，指因某事而感到被认可或感谢。

14.original: Next morning, lo and behold, when I stepped into the elevator I heard, Good morning, Dr. Schwartz.
14.b1: The next morning, when I got into the elevator, she said, "Good morning, Dr. Schwartz."
14.cn: 第二天早上，当我走进电梯时，竟然听到了她说：“早上好，施瓦茨博士。”
14.ai_notes:
'lo and behold': an expression used to show surprise at something that happened.
'stepped into': to walk into a small space.
14.remarks:
'lo and behold': 你瞧，用于表达对某事发生的惊讶。
'stepped into': 走进，指步入一个小空间。

15.original: Not one time before had I heard this operator address anyone by name.
15.b1: She had never called anyone by their name before.
15.cn: 在此之前，我从未听过这位操作员叫过任何人的名字。
15.ai_notes: 'address': to speak to someone, often using their name or title.
15.remarks: 'address': 称呼，指用名字或头衔与某人交谈。

16.original: And in the remaining months that I had an office in the building,
16.b1: And for the rest of the time I had an office in that building,
16.cn: 而在我还在那栋楼里办公的几个月里，
16.ai_notes: 'remaining months': the time left in a specific period.
16.remarks: 'remaining months': 剩余的月份，指特定时期内剩下的时间。

17.original: I never heard anyone called by name except me.
17.b1: I never heard her call anyone else by their name.
17.cn: 我再也没听过她叫过其他人的名字。

18.original: I had made the operator feel important.
18.b1: I had made her feel important.
18.cn: 我让她感到了自己的重要性。
18.ai_notes: 'made the operator feel important': caused her to feel that she was a person of value.
18.remarks: 'made the operator feel important': 让操作员感到被重视，指让她觉得自己的存在是有价值的。

19.original: I had sincerely complimented her and called her by name.
19.b1: I gave her a sincere compliment and used her name.
19.cn: 我真诚地赞美了她，并叫出了她的名字。
19.remarks: 'sincerely': 真诚地，指诚实且带有真实情感。

20.original: I had made her feel important.
20.b1: I made her feel that she mattered.
20.cn: 我让她觉得自己很重要。
20.remarks: 'mattered': 重要，指具有意义或重要性。

21.original: Now she was repaying me by making me feel important.
21.b1: Now she was making me feel important in return.
21.cn: 现在，她通过让我感到重要来回报我。
21.ai_notes: 'repaying': to do something good for someone because they did something good for you.
21.remarks: 'repaying': 回报，指因为别人对你好而做些好事作为回馈。

22.ai_notes: 'kid ourselves': to believe something that is not true to make ourselves feel better.
22.remarks: 'kid ourselves': 自欺欺人，指为了让自己好受而相信不真实的事情。

23.original: People who do not have a deep-down feeling of self-importance are slated for mediocrity.
23.b1: People who do not feel important will not achieve much.
23.cn: 那些内心深处没有自我重要感的人，注定平庸。
23.ai_notes:
'deep-down': a feeling that is very strong and inside a person's heart.
'slated for': likely to experience or become something.
'mediocrity': the quality of being average or not very good.
23.remarks:
'deep-down': 内心深处，指内心强烈的感觉。
'slated for': 注定要，指很可能经历或成为某种状态。
'mediocrity': 平庸，指处于平均水平或表现不佳。

24.original: Again and again, this point must be driven home.
24.ai_notes: 'driven home': to make a point very clear and easy to understand.
24.remarks: 'driven home': 强调，指把某个观点阐述得非常清晰易懂。

25.original: You must feel important to succeed.
25.b1: You need to feel important to be successful.
25.cn: 你必须感到自己很重要才能成功。
25.ai_notes: 'succeed': to achieve what you want or aim for.
25.remarks: 'succeed': 成功，指达成目标或愿望。

26.original: Helping others to feel important rewards you because it makes you feel more important.
26.b1: Helping others feel important is good for you because it makes you feel better too.
26.cn: 帮助他人感到重要会让你受益，因为它也会让你感觉更好。
26.ai_notes: 'rewards': to give something good in return for an action.
26.remarks: 'rewards': 奖励，指因某项行为而给予的好处。

27.ai_notes: 'Try it and see': a phrase used to encourage someone to test an idea for themselves.
27.remarks: 'Try it and see': 试一试就知道了，鼓励他人亲自验证某个想法。

28.b1: Here is how to do it.
28.ai_notes: 'Here's how to do it': a common introduction to a list of instructions.
28.remarks: 'Here's how to do it': 操作方法如下，介绍步骤的常用语。

29.ai_notes: '1.': The start of a list of steps.
29.remarks: '1.': 第一点，列表的起始标记。

30.ai_notes: 'Practice appreciation': to make a habit of showing thanks.
30.remarks: 'Practice appreciation': 练习感恩，指养成表达感谢的习惯。

31.original: Make it a rule to let others know you appreciate what they do for you.
31.b1: Make it a rule to tell people you appreciate what they do.
31.cn: 养成一个习惯，让别人知道你感激他们为你所做的一切。
31.ai_notes: 'Make it a rule': to decide to do something consistently.
31.remarks: 'Make it a rule': 定为准则，指决定始终如一地执行某事。

32.original: Never, never let anyone feel he is taken for granted.
32.b1: Never make anyone feel that you do not care about them.
32.cn: 永远不要让任何人觉得他们是被忽视的。
32.ai_notes: 'taken for granted': to be treated as if you are not important or as if your help is expected without thanks.
32.remarks: 'taken for granted': 被视为理所当然，指被忽视重要性或帮助被认为是不需要感谢的。

33.original: Practice appreciation with a warm, sincere smile.
33.b1: Show appreciation with a warm, honest smile.
33.cn: 用温暖、真诚的微笑来表达感激。

34.original: A smile lets others know you notice them and feel kindly toward them.
34.b1: A smile shows that you notice people and like them.
34.cn: 微笑让别人知道你注意到了他们，并对他们怀有善意。
34.ai_notes: 'feel kindly toward': to have friendly or good feelings about someone.
34.remarks: 'feel kindly toward': 对某人有好感，指对某人怀有友善的情绪。

35.original: Practice appreciation by letting others know how you depend on them.
35.b1: Show appreciation by telling people how much you need them.
35.cn: 通过告诉别人你有多依赖他们来表达感激。
35.ai_notes: 'depend on': to need someone's help to do something.
35.remarks: 'depend on': 依赖，指需要某人的帮助来完成某事。

36.original: An earnest, Jim, I don't know what we'd do without you,
36.b1: Saying something like, "Jim, I don't know what I would do without you,"
36.cn: 说一句真诚的“吉姆，我真不知道没有你我该怎么办”，
36.remarks: 'earnest': 认真的，指严肃且真诚的态度。

37.original: type of remark makes people feel necessary, and when they feel necessary, they do increasingly better work.
37.b1: makes people feel necessary, and then they work even better.
37.cn: 会让人们感到自己不可或缺，而当他们感到被需要时，工作会做得越来越好。
37.ai_notes:
'remark': a comment or something said.
'necessary': needed or essential.
'increasingly': more and more.
37.remarks:
'remark': 评论，指所说的话。
'necessary': 必要的，指不可或缺的。
'increasingly': 越来越，表示程度加深。

38.original: Practice appreciation with honest, personalized compliments.
38.b1: Show appreciation with honest and personal compliments.
38.cn: 用真诚且个性化的赞美来表达感激。
38.remarks: 'personalized': 个性化的，指专门为某人定制的。

39.original: People thrive on compliments, whether 2 or 20, 9 or 90.
39.b1: Everyone likes compliments, no matter how old they are.
39.cn: 人们都喜欢赞美，无论他们是2岁还是90岁。
39.ai_notes: 'thrive on': to grow or feel very good because of something.
39.remarks: 'thrive on': 从中获益，指因某事而成长或感觉良好。

40.ai_notes: 'craves': to have a very strong desire for something.
40.remarks: 'craves': 渴望，指对某事物有强烈的需求。

41.original: He wants to be assured that he's doing a good job, that he is important.
41.b1: They want to know they are doing a good job and that they are important.
41.cn: 他们想要得到肯定，知道自己工作出色，且很重要。
41.ai_notes: 'assured': to be told something to make you feel confident.
41.remarks: 'assured': 被保证，指被告知某事以获得信心。

42.original: Don't feel that you should hand out praise only for big accomplishments.
42.b1: Do not only praise people for big things.
42.cn: 不要只在别人取得重大成就时才给予赞美。
42.ai_notes: 'hand out': to give something to others.
42.remarks: 'hand out': 分发，指把东西给别人。

43.original: Compliment people on little things, their appearance, the way they do their routine work, their ideas, their loyal efforts.
43.b1: Compliment them on small things, like their appearance, their work, or their ideas.
43.cn: 赞美他们的小事，比如外表、日常工作表现、想法或忠诚的努力。
43.ai_notes:
'routine work': the regular tasks you do every day.
'loyal efforts': hard work done with dedication.
43.remarks:
'routine work': 日常工作，指每天做的常规任务。
'loyal efforts': 忠诚的付出，指全心全意的努力。

44.original: Praise by writing personal notes complimenting people you know on their achievements.
44.b1: Write personal notes to praise people for what they have done.
44.cn: 写私人便条来赞美人们的成就。
44.remarks: 'personal notes': 个人便条，指简短的手写信息。

45.original: Make a special phone call or a special trip to see them.
45.b1: Make a special phone call or visit them.
45.cn: 打个特别的电话或专门去拜访他们。
45.ai_notes: 'special trip': traveling somewhere specifically to see someone.
45.remarks: 'special trip': 特别行程，指专门为了见某人而进行的旅行。

46.original: Don't waste time or mental energy trying to classify people as very important persons, important persons, or unimportant persons.
46.b1: Don't waste time trying to decide who is important and who is not.
46.cn: 不要浪费时间和精力去把人分为重要人物或不重要人物。
46.ai_notes: 'classify': to group things or people into categories.
46.remarks: 'classify': 分类，指将人或事物归入不同类别。

47.ai_notes: 'Make no exceptions': to treat everyone the same way without any differences.
47.remarks: 'Make no exceptions': 一视同仁，指对待所有人都不搞特殊。

48.original: A person, whether he is garbage collector or company vice president, is important to you.
48.b1: Everyone is important to you, whether they are a cleaner or a boss.
48.cn: 每个人对你来说都很重要，无论他是清洁工还是公司副总裁。
48.ai_notes: 'garbage collector': a person whose job is to take away trash.
48.remarks: 'garbage collector': 清洁工，指负责清理垃圾的人。

49.original: Treating someone as second class never gets you first class results.
49.b1: If you treat people badly, you will not get good results.
49.cn: 把别人当成二等公民对待，永远不会让你得到一流的结果。
49.ai_notes:
'second class': treated as less important than others.
'first class results': the best possible outcomes.
49.remarks:
'second class': 二等，指被视为不如别人重要。
'first class results': 一流成果，指尽可能最好的结果。
